_meta.description: Common business expense deductions for self-employed workers in Quebec, organized by business type. Helps Claude suggest missing expense categories in the Financial Snapshot.

title_en: Business Expense Deductions for Self-Employed Workers
title_fr: Déductions pour dépenses d'entreprise — Travailleurs autonomes
plain_language_summary: As a self-employed person, you can deduct legitimate business expenses from your income before calculating tax. This directly reduces what you owe. Most new self-employed workers miss deductions — especially home office, vehicle, phone, and professional development. Track everything from day one.

universal_deductions.note: These apply to almost all self-employed business types

categories.name: Home Office Expenses
categories.plain_language: If you use part of your home exclusively and regularly for business, you can deduct a proportional amount of your housing costs.
what_qualifies: Rent (proportional % of your space used for work)
Internet (business use portion — typically 50-80% for most self-employed)
Home insurance (if you've notified insurer of business use)
Mortgage interest (proportional — for homeowners, not principal)
categories.how_to_calculate: Measure your dedicated work area in square feet. Divide by total home square footage. That % applies to shared housing costs. Example: 200 sq ft office / 1,000 sq ft home = 20% deductible.
categories.important_limitation: Home office deductions cannot create a business loss — they can only reduce income to zero, not below. Carry forward any excess.
categories.form: CRA Form T2125 (Statement of Business Activities), Revenu Québec TP-80-V

categories.name: Phone and Internet
categories.plain_language: The business-use portion of your phone and internet bills is deductible.
categories.what_qualifies: Monthly bills, data plans, apps used for business
categories.how_to_calculate: Estimate business vs personal use %. Common approach: 50-80% for most self-employed who use phone primarily for work.
categories.tip: Having a separate business phone line makes this 100% deductible with no calculation needed

categories.name: Professional Development
what_qualifies: Online courses and subscriptions (Udemy, Coursera, LinkedIn Learning)
Books and publications related to your field
categories.montreal_specific: YES Montréal workshops, PME MTL training — may be deductible if business-related

categories.name: Marketing and Advertising
what_qualifies: Social media advertising (Meta Ads, Google Ads)

categories.name: Legal and Professional Fees
what_qualifies: Legal advice related to the business

categories.name: Business Insurance
categories.important: Personal life insurance is NOT deductible

categories.name: Office Supplies and Equipment
categories.cca_note: Equipment over $500 (computers, cameras, furniture) is typically deducted over multiple years via CCA (Capital Cost Allowance), not all at once

categories.name: Meals and Entertainment
categories.what_qualifies: Meals or entertainment with clients or business associates
categories.deductible_portion: 50% only — both federal and Quebec limit this to 50%
categories.record_keeping: Keep receipt AND note: who attended, business purpose

specific_deductions.name: Ingredients and Food Supplies
specific_deductions.plain_language: The cost of ingredients used to make your products is a direct business expense.

specific_deductions.name: Commercial Kitchen Rental
specific_deductions.plain_language: If you rent a commercial kitchen for production, the rental fee is fully deductible.
montreal_options: Various church/community kitchens available for hourly rental

specific_deductions.name: Food Safety Training
specific_deductions.plain_language: MAPAQ food safety training and any food safety certifications are deductible.

specific_deductions.name: Market and Vendor Fees
specific_deductions.plain_language: Farmers' market stall fees, event vendor fees, and market membership fees are deductible.

specific_deductions.name: Specialized Equipment
examples: Stand mixer, food processor, baking pans
specific_deductions.note: Items over $500 typically use CCA (Capital Cost Allowance)

specific_deductions.name: Delivery and Transportation
specific_deductions.plain_language: Gas and vehicle use for delivering orders or going to markets. Track mileage for each business trip.
specific_deductions.how_to_track: Keep a mileage log: date, destination, business purpose, km driven

freelance.label: Freelancer / Consultant (Software, Design, Writing, etc.)

specific_deductions.name: Computer and Hardware
specific_deductions.plain_language: Your laptop, monitor, keyboard, and other equipment used for work.
specific_deductions.cca_note: Computers qualify for 55% CCA rate (Class 10) — meaning you deduct 55% of the cost in year 1, then 55% of the remaining balance in subsequent years
specific_deductions.immediate_expensing: As of 2023+, self-employed individuals may be eligible for immediate full expensing of eligible depreciable property in the year of acquisition — verify with accountant

specific_deductions.name: Subcontractor Costs
specific_deductions.plain_language: If you hire other freelancers to help with a project, their fees are deductible. You must issue a T4A slip if you pay more than $500 to a single person in a year.

specific_deductions.name: Home Office
specific_deductions.plain_language: Most freelancers work primarily from home — home office deduction is significant and commonly missed.

commonly_missed: Subcontractor T4A slips (not a deduction but a compliance requirement)

specific_deductions.name: Toys and Educational Materials
specific_deductions.plain_language: Toys, books, art supplies, learning materials purchased for the children in your care are deductible.

specific_deductions.name: Food and Snacks for Children
specific_deductions.plain_language: Food you provide to children in your care is a business expense.
specific_deductions.how_to_calculate: Keep grocery receipts and estimate the portion used for daycare children vs your family

specific_deductions.name: Home Modifications for Safety
examples: Safety gates, outlet covers, cabinet locks
specific_deductions.note: Major modifications (structural changes) use CCA; small safety items are current expenses

specific_deductions.name: Home Insurance — Business Rider
specific_deductions.plain_language: If you've added a home daycare rider to your home insurance (you should), that premium is deductible.

specific_deductions.name: Home Office and Space Used for Daycare
specific_deductions.plain_language: The area of your home used for daycare (play area, nap area, etc.) can be deducted proportionally from housing costs.
specific_deductions.special_rule: For home daycares, CRA allows a more generous calculation — verify with an accountant specializing in RSG tax returns

specific_deductions.name: Cost of Goods Sold (COGS)
specific_deductions.plain_language: The cost of products you purchase for resale is deductible.
specific_deductions.formula: Opening inventory + Purchases during year - Closing inventory = Cost of Goods Sold

specific_deductions.name: Shipping and Postage
specific_deductions.plain_language: Costs to ship products to customers or receive inventory.

specific_deductions.name: Packaging Materials
specific_deductions.plain_language: Boxes, bags, tissue paper, tape — everything used to package products for sale or shipping.

specific_deductions.name: Booth Rental or Studio Rental
specific_deductions.plain_language: If you rent a booth at a salon or a studio space, that's fully deductible.
specific_deductions.montreal_context: Many stylists and estheticians rent booths — this is a significant deduction

specific_deductions.name: Professional Certifications
specific_deductions.plain_language: Renewal fees for professional permits and certifications are deductible.

specific_deductions.name: Liability Insurance
specific_deductions.plain_language: Professional liability insurance is essential and deductible for personal care services.

record_keeping_requirements.how_long_to_keep: 6 years from the end of the tax year they relate to
digital_tools: Wave Accounting (free — popular with Quebec micro-entrepreneurs)
A simple Excel/Google Sheets log is acceptable for small operations
record_keeping_requirements.tip: Take a photo of every receipt immediately — paper fades. Apps like Wave let you scan receipts with your phone.

what_is_NOT_deductible: Personal expenses (groceries, personal clothing, personal trips)
Fines and penalties (parking tickets, CRA penalties)
Loan principal payments (interest is deductible, principal is not)
Club memberships (unless exclusively for business client entertainment — rare)
professional_advice: For complex deductions or large purchases, consult a CPA. Many offer free initial consultations. Community tax clinics (CVITP) serve low-income self-employed individuals for free.
